So the problem says that when S(n) is divided by (n + 5), the remainder is 17. So in mathematical terms, this is S(n) ≡ 17 mod (n + 5). Which means that S(n) - 17 is divisible by (n + 5). Therefore, (n + 5) divides [S(n) - 17]. Alternatively, we can write S(n) mod (n + 5) = 17. Since remainders are non-negative and less than the divisor, we know that 17 must be less than (n + 5). Therefore, n + 5 must be greater than 17, so n + 5 > 17 ⇒ n > 12. Therefore, all solutions n must be integers greater than 12. So n ≥ 13. But let me confirm that.
